Title: Arabidopsis HOOKLESS1 acts as a histone acetyltransferase to promote cotyledon greening during seedling de-etiolation
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enables photosynthesis and autotrophic growth during seedling de-etiolation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cotyledon greening, chlorophyll accumulation, Pchlide-to-Chlide transition, ROS scavenging capacity, and chloroplast development; loss of HLS1 causes ROS burst and photobleaching upon light exposure
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: chlorophyll biosynthesis; chloroplast development; ROS detoxification-associated greening program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates greening-related genes by depositing H3K9ac and H3K27ac at their regulatory regions; facilitates EIN3 binding to PORA and PORB promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator of chlorophyll accumulation and cotyledon greening during dark-to-light transition
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light-to-dark transition state; ethylene signaling via EIN3/EIL1; ACC treatment context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HOOKLESS1 family protein with acetyltransferase activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: HLS1 is strongly upregulated in etiolated seedlings grown in darkness and downregulated during de-etiolation/light exposure
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in dark-grown etiolated seedlings; functionally important during early de-etiolation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolated seedlings grown 4-6 d in darkness followed by white light exposure; ACC (10 μM) treatment; β-estrogen induction
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes severe photobleaching and ROS burst during de-etiolation; no other yield penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant (hls1-1); complementation/overexpression; inducible expression; chlorophyll and Pchlide/Chlide measurements; ROS imaging; mRNA-seq; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; in vitro histone acetylation assay; in vivo acetylation assay
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: HLS1 itself is the epigenetic effector; it deposits H3K9ac and H3K27ac at greening-related loci, and loss of HLS1 reduces these marks genome-wide
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HLS1 is essential for light-induced cotyledon greening in etiolated Arabidopsis seedlings. 2. HLS1 promotes chlorophyll accumulation by supporting the Pchlide-to-Chlide transition and chloroplast maturation. 3. HLS1 acts as a bona fide histone acetyltransferase that increases H3K9ac and H3K27ac at greening-related genes including PORA and PORB. 4. HLS1 is required for the ethylene-promoted activation of POR genes by facilitating EIN3 binding.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance seedling establishment and de-etiolation robustness by engineering chromatin-based activation of chlorophyll biosynthesis genes; caution that broad chromatin effects may cause pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The promotive greening role is dependent on HLS1 acetyltransferase activity; HLS1(LV) mutant version with reduced acetyltransferase activity fails to fully rescue greening. ATG18a is not required for this greening phenotype.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enables photosynthesis and autotrophic growth during seedling de-etiolation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene-mediated cotyledon greening through activation of POR genes; binding/activity is reduced in hls1 background
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ethylene-responsive activation of chlorophyll biosynthesis during greening
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds EBS-containing promoter regions of PORA and PORB and activates their expression; this binding depends on HLS1-mediated histone acetylation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ethylene-response regulator that promotes chlorophyll biosynthetic transition during de-etiolation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with EIL1 in ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ACC treatment; dark-grown seedlings transferred to light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ACC treatment assays; ein3 eil1 mutant analysis; ChIP-qPCR for binding to PORA/PORB; RT-qPCR of PORA/PORB expression
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Binding to PORA/PORB is facilitated by HLS1-mediated H3 acetylation at target loci
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIN3 positively mediates ethylene-promoted cotyledon greening through activation of POR genes. 2. EIN3 enrichment at PORA and PORB promoters is strongly reduced in hls1-1. 3. HLS1-dependent histone acetylation is required for full EIN3 transcriptional activation of PORs during greening.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be combined with chromatin-level activators such as HLS1 to strengthen ethylene-promoted seedling greening, but broad ethylene effects may constrain deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper EIN3 is included because its binding to POR genes was directly tested in the Results as part of the HLS1-dependent mechanism.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enables photosynthesis and autotrophic growth during seedling de-etiolation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with EIN3 to ethylene-promoted cotyledon greening, inferred from ein3 eil1 mutant phenotype used in this study
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ethylene-responsive regulation of chlorophyll biosynthesis during greening
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with EIN3/EIL1 module to promote POR expression during greening
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive ethylene-signaling regulator for greening, but less directly characterized than EIN3 in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with EIN3 in ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ACC treatment; dark-grown seedlings transferred to light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ein3 eil1 mutant phenotyping under ACC during greening
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Likely indirectly dependent on HLS1-mediated histone acetylation at POR loci, but not directly tested for EIL1 binding in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIL1 participates with EIN3 in ethylene-promoted greening, as shown using the ein3 eil1 mutant. 2. The study mainly focuses mechanistically on EIN3 rather than EIL1.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary regulatory target in ethylene-greening engineering, likely best considered with EIN3 and HLS1 rather than alone
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the double mutant ein3 eil1 was directly analyzed in Results, although direct molecular assays centered on EIN3.
